title: Docs Drift Checker
purpose: Detect obvious drift between the runnable routine command surface, JSON routine specs, key user docs, roadmap status text, and accepted/merged task review notes without mutating repository state.
trigger: Run after adding or changing routine runners, CLI command help, routine specs, roadmap/status docs, or accepting/merging a task that may require orchestrator-owned central docs updates.
allowedActions: parse the run-routine command surface from source
scan key docs for runnable routine references
scan accepted or merged review notes for central-impact changes missing an explicit docs-drift decision
write a RoutineRunReport JSON when requested
forbiddenActions: stage, commit, merge, push, tag, or release
dispatch sessions or start nested subagents
claim direct runtime proof from static docs inspection
gates: each runnable routine has a JSON spec
README.md, README.zh-CN.md, SKILL.md, docs/routines/README.md, and docs/v2-usage.md mention each runnable routine
docs/roadmap.md checked for runnable routine mentions and stale status text when present
docs/reviews checked for accepted/merged central-impact task notes missing central docs update or docs-drift decision

evidence.direct: Reserved for future doc-render or user-facing publication proof; the MVP does not emit direct proof.
evidence.proxy: Reserved for accepted external docs indexes or release notes; the MVP normally does not emit proxy proof.
evidence.local: Local source, routine spec, key documentation, roadmap, and review-note inspection.
evidence.blocked: Repository, source, routine spec directory, optional roadmap, or review docs cannot be inspected safely.

escalation: Stop if repository or source files cannot be inspected.
Do not edit docs from this routine; report drift and let a separate implementation pass fix it.
Escalate if roadmap status requires product or release-history judgment beyond directly observable local text.
